sources.text: What AI tools did you use? ❖ OpenAI’s ChatGPT ➢ DALL·E 3 ❖ Google’s Gemini ➢ Nano Banana 2 ❖ Visual Studio Code (VS Code) ❖ Suno AI ➢ Suno’s built-in “Write Full Song” AI Specific prompts/tasks Image prompt excerpts DALL·E 3 Google’s Gemini “Generate a comic panel of a raccoon and squirrel meeting each other in the woods and hugging.” “Keep everything the same in the image about the man. It should not look different. This means the selfie, the angle, and everything. The only changes are to dress him up in realistic Roman soldier clothing” “Continue the comic panel with another image that shows the raccoon feeling bad for stealing and returning the cherries while apologizing.” N/A Standard text prompt excerpts OpenAI’s ChatGPT Google’s Gemini (Comparison with Voyant tool) “How do I combine these CSV files to Suno AI Prompt [Radio Static] (Radio Voice): “This is Prometheus, come in Daemon. Daemon, do you read?” (Field Op): “Extraction point Orkney is compromised. Need info on my extraction.” What you kept vs. changed ❖ Unless required by the assignment, I changed most responses of AI to fit what I used it for. In all of these cases, I probably could have gotten a similar result searching up the prompt on the internet and reading websites for answers. A lot of the image prompts were “Provide a literary analysis of the Homeric Hymns, focusing on symbols, themes, imagery, and any other literary elements you find important.” import into Kumu? One is Nodes the other is Edges.” “Give sources and tell me of your rival, Google's Gemini, about affecting Mining sites and content moderation.” “Can you help me find sources for OpenAI's ChatGPT in any of these categories? 1. Content Moderation & Data Labeling Labor, 2. Affecting Mining Sites” “What is a fitting top text to add to a meme gif depicting the Fabian strategy involving its creation?” N/A “Generate a smartphone selfie of a 19-year-old male college student whose ethnic makeup is summarized as being half- Bohemian/Czech from his mother's side and half Italian from his father's side. He has a medium build, a mix between a fair and olive skin tone, right-parted dark brown hair, and eyes that are a hazel with a tint of green.” N/A slightly edited to continue a story to be used in Make 3. For Make 8, the Suno “Write Full Song” I recycled my prompts with added additions of quotes or related subject words to the Frankenstein novel. From both of these examples, some aspects are kept from the AI’s generation, but its direction was ordered by me. Your policy on AI use ❖ Let AI act as my legion while I, the general, create ideas and dictate direction. In other words, ignore AI suggestions and edit additions when necessary until I hit a road block and require some feedback to proceed.

Sack 1 Brendan Sack Dr. Francisco López-Martín DH101 - Critical Making in the Age of AI 29 March 2026 Make 8. Build a bot or generator using a no-code AI tool 1. The Generator (or documentation of it) Above is a screenshot of Suno’s create music tab. On the left side, there is an option between “Simple” and “Advanced.” The “Simple” tab allows for a simple prompt to turn into a song while “Advanced” allows you to input specific lyrics, styles, weirdness, and vocal gender. Some of these features are locked behind a paywall, so I just utilized the Lyrics and Styles section. Sack 2 2. Curated Outputs (5-10 for text, 3-5 for images/music) EP Title: //PROJECT_PROMETHEUS// Genre: Frenchcore, Speedcore, Hardstyle 1. ORKNEY_COMPROMISED (first try, best try) This was the first track that I generated. The lyrics in this track, although short and reused, are what I based every other track’s lyrics on. The fact that it did not become too distorted and almost sounds like stock audio is why I decided to select it. 2. PHYSIOGNOMY_OF_WAR (my favorite lyrics) I wrote the first track’s lyrics myself and used the built-in “Write Full Song” AI to generate new lyrics. In between responses, I added different words, quotes, and phrases relating to the Frankenstein novel. This track’s lyrics felt very fitting to a narcissist villain mastermind, so I decided to include it. 3. MONSTRUM_PROTOCOL (a little ear rapey) Not a lot of the tracks generated had a female vocal in them and the frantic beat generated convinced me to select it. It is a little ear rapey, but I found it fits into an electrical-related sound that worked well with the beat. 4. DAEMON_SILENCE (favorite beat) This is a remix/sequel with added lyrics of the first track. I like the first track a lot but felt it could do with a little more lyrics. In the end, this track almost felt like a second part of the first track that helped inspire me to create a sort of narrative between tracks. Sack 3 3. Process Documentation (~350 words, excluding the example prompts listed) I originally followed Track A: Text Generator but felt dissatisfied with the results, so I decided to follow Track C: Music/Sound Generator and chose Suno. Coming into it, I knew I wanted the tracks to be hardcore techno as it matched the kinda crazy rapidly evolving aspect felt in the genre and AI development. Suno has an option between two tabs: “Simple” which takes a worded prompt and “Advanced” which takes Lyrics and Styles inputs for a free account. I wanted to write my own lyrics for at least the first track, so I decided to go with the “Advanced,” despite some limited options. I controlled both the Lyrics and Styles parameters used in generating the music. The Styles parameter were not changed beyond what was first inputted. The input contained a list of styles that I searched all over the internet and thought of myself: “Extreme speedcore, aggressive Frenchcore, 155 BPM, aggressive percussion, high-tempo action, military extraction survival vibe, dark cinematic atmosphere, industrial-tinged electronic thrilling, dark metallic textures, distorted/radio-filtered, action-oriented electronic” The Lyrics parameter is what I changed the most. I started with this radio call that I wrote myself.: [Radio Static] (Radio Voice): “This is Prometheus, come in Daemon. Daemon, do you read?” (Field Op): “Extraction point Orkney is compromised. Need info on my extraction.” Originally, the lyrics were generic military communications, but I decided to make references to Mary Shelley’s Frankenstein because it honestly felt like I was creating a grotesque monster when adding my own lyrics to an AI song. For the rest of the song’s lyrics, I used Suno’s “Write Full Song” AI to remix and create lyrics from my prompts. For this process, I followed a part mentioned in Chapter 7 of Critical Sack 4 Making in the Age of AI by Anastasia Salter and Emily Johnson. When discussing applications for generated text, it is mentioned “Writing sentences and paragraphs with potential meanings rather than singular definitive statements is a creative endeavor that can be frustrating…” Although minor in the text, I took this idea to heart and went through this process of recycling responses and adding new words or phrases relating to Frankenstein until I felt the lyrical track was good enough. This process sometimes lasted a few to a dozen times, but I endured. Words and phrases I added were: ❖ Victor, Monstrum, Reanimated, Experiment, Galvanize, Charnel house, Physiognomy, Terror, Chimera, Theseus, ALIVE! ❖ “I shall be with you on your wedding night”, “Beware; for I am fearless, and therefore powerful”, “I was benevolent and good; misery made me a fiend”, “No one can conceive the variety of feelings which bore me onwards, like a hurricane, in the first enthusiasm of success.” Sack 5 In total, I generated a total of twelve tracks based on six Lyrics prompts. This is because Suno generates two tracks based on one generation. 4. Artist Statement (~265 words) The creative aspect when it comes to AI generation comes in the form of knowing when to choose and stop. As Chapter 7 of Critical Making in the Age of AI critiqued the idea of an unbiased code, the AI presents its challenging decisions that reflect biases of its sources. Depending on what is wanted by the user, this loop of generation can be stopped. What can be wanted by the user can be different depending on the specific user’s tastes and objectives aka their own biases. The result of this conflict with the user stopping the AI’s generation reveals the creativity input. The most clear human process in my generated songs is the lyrical choices and overall genre of the songs. The genre of hardcore techno was specifically selected by me and not a machine. I wrote the first track’s lyrics and based on a process of adding, editing, and deleting responses from the “Write Full Song” AI, I selected which outputs fit. Therefore, the first track’s lyrics and the edits of the other tracks were made by me and not the machine. I decided what was “good” from Suno’s AI generation based on a short criterion. First, it must contain words that were added by myself. Second, the AI voices must match the rhythm of the song. If any track’s AI voice sounded too unnatural, the song was instantly disregarded. Lastly, the beat must be acceptable to the ears. I did not want my ears to bleed after each listen. Most tracks passed the first and third aspect, but the second was frequently missed. Sack 6 Attribution ★ AI Tools used — Suno AI ○ Suno’s built-in “Write Full Song” AI ★ Additional AI assistance disclosed — I searched on the internet “Unique words used in Frankenstein by Mary Shelley” and may have been inspired to use info found in Google’s AI overview. ★ Website viewed — I searched the following website for lyric inspiration and refreshers on Frankenstein : ○ https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/Frankenstein_(novel) ○ https://www.goodreads.com/work/quotes/4836639-frankenstein-or-the-modern-pr ometheus ○ https://www.litcharts.com/lit/frankenstein/characters ○ (PDF File of Vocab) https://mhsliterature.weebly.com/uploads/3/0/3/9/3039381/frankensteinvocabular ylistanddefinitions.pdf
